article_meta.title: Once upon a time, school students would submit an essay, and teachers would mark it

reasoning: Mixed performance: it correctly flags author-prose spin and subjective adjectives, but commits an attribution violation by flagging a quoted warning as author bias and misses at least one clear unsourced claim in the author's voice.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Flags Catt’s quoted language as author bias, violating the attribution rule; other items are from author prose.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: One of four items is a clear false positive (quoted text treated as author bias); the rest are defensible.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Misses at least one notable author-level bias: 'Early results show it has saved time...' lacks source attribution; also could have flagged 'hammering in the lesson.'
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels on the valid items are appropriate (Spin; Subjective Qualifying Adjectives; Omission of Source Attribution).
